session_hint: the JWT authentication random logout investigation
question: What was causing users to be randomly logged out in the mobile app?
turns: Mobile app users are getting 401 responses randomly, 10-20 minutes after logging in. JWT tokens are issued with a 24h expiry so they shouldn't be expiring. First theory: the token blacklist Redis store is returning false positives and marking valid tokens as revoked.
Added debug logging to the blacklist middleware. For every affected 401, the token ID is NOT present in Redis. The blacklist is not touching these tokens. Ruling out false-positive blacklisting.

Second theory: CORS preflight is rejecting the Authorization header for certain API routes, causing the client to silently fail. Checked nginx CORS logs and the browser network tab for affected sessions — Authorization header is accepted on all preflight checks, no CORS rejections. Ruling out CORS.

Compared server clocks across services. The auth-service EC2 instance is 97 seconds ahead of the api-gateway EC2 instance — NTP sync failed on auth-service after a kernel upgrade and was never caught. JWT tokens have an iat (issued-at) claim set to auth-service time. api-gateway validates tokens with a 60-second leeway. A token issued at T+97s looks like it was issued 97 seconds in the future from api-gateway's perspective — 37 seconds outside the leeway window — so all tokens from that auth-service are rejected immediately on arrival. Fixed by forcing `chronyc makestep` on auth-service. Root cause: NTP clock drift of 97 seconds making tokens appear issued in the future.
